proposed_label: Idioms with an obligatory frozen tail (mettercela tutta)
proposed_description: A small class of advanced set-idioms built on a pronominal stem plus an obligatory frozen tail that the context must supply: mettercela TUTTA (to give it your all), darsela A GAMBE (to leg it), legarsela AL DITO (to bear a grudge), tirarla PER LE LUNGHE (to drag it out), dirne DI TUTTI I COLORI (to give someone an earful). The stem carries the pronominal mechanics (frozen la, feminine participle); the tail is fixed lexical material, not a slot the learner fills freely.

proposed_attributes.canonical_example: Ce l'ho messa tutta. / Se l'e data a gambe.
proposed_attributes.common_miss: Agreeing the frozen tail with the subject ('ce l'ho messa tutto') or omitting it.

rationale: Needed while authoring pv_adv_frozentail_01 (mettercela tutta), which I parked on mechanics.sela_type as the nearest home. These idioms are a coherent C1 cluster (the OIC drill flags them as the strongest advanced mechanic) and would carry a proper coverage pass of their own; the frozen-TAIL agreement is a distinct diagnostic from the frozen-clitic agreement the sela_type leaf already owns. Proposed under my own tree per the cite-not-duplicate contract; not created here, flagged for the architect.
